summary: Tested promo codes (INK50), reference-character consistency, low-credit upsell, char-consistency toggle gating, walking doodle animation, and Reader PDF export. Backend: 30/30 pytest passing (23 regression + 7 new). Frontend: 6/7 flows pass — walking doodle + best-plan-card lift OK; promo INK50 shows strikethrough + $4.00 + applied badge; low-credits upsell renders for free user with 3 credits; char-consistency-toggle disabled for free, enabled for Pro/Ultimate; credits-remaining shows 'Unlimited sketches' for Ultimate. CRITICAL: reader-pdf button is NOT rendered in Reader.jsx — downloadPDF function exists (lines 37-93) but no <button data-testid='reader-pdf'> is wired to it.

ui_bugs.issue: reader-pdf button is NOT rendered in the JSX. downloadPDF() function is fully implemented (lines 37-93 with jsPDF, Ultimate gating toast, and grid layout) but the button to invoke it is missing from the toolbar (only reader-download for HTML exists). Feature is half-wired — backend/logic done, UI button missing.

action_items: HIGH: Add a <button data-testid='reader-pdf' onClick={downloadPDF}> in Reader.jsx toolbar (around line 144-147, next to reader-download). Use FileDown icon already imported. Label e.g. 'PDF' with Crown hint for non-Ultimate. Without this button, PDF export feature is inaccessible via UI on non-Ultimate AND Ultimate tiers.
(optional) consider disabling state during exportingPdf to prevent double-clicks.
critical_code_review_comments: Reader.jsx L37-93: downloadPDF is complete but unreachable — no button invokes it. exportingPdf state is also unused in UI.

server.py L336: reference_image_b64 is silently ignored for free users (no 402/403) — good UX but undocumented. Consider adding a response flag 'reference_used': true/false so frontend can show 'reference applied' indicator only for Pro/Ultimate.
server.py PROMO_CODES L446-448 is a static dict — fine for MVP but consider moving to DB for non-engineer management and audit.
server.py /billing/checkout L500: first_time check uses payment_status='paid' which is correct, but the granted flag is a stronger invariant — consider OR-ing both.
WalkingDoodle.jsx is CSS-only with fixed 9s timeline; users on slow devices may miss the lift. Consider using Intersection Observer to start animation when billing page is in view.
Reader.jsx jsPDF bundled into main app bundle — consider dynamic import to cut initial bundle size since feature is Ultimate-only.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_billing.py (one assertion relaxed from ==20 to <=20 to handle test-order credit deduction)
test_credentials: FREE: test_session_1776681630005 (user=test-user-1776681630005, credits~18, tier=free). LOW-CREDIT FREE: demo_1776682698377 (3 credits, tier=free) — for upsell banner. PRO (new, seeded this iter): test_session_pro_1776682700000 (tier=pro, expires in 30d). ULT: test_session_ult_1776681630019 (tier=ultimate, expires 2026-05-20). ZERO: test_session_zero_1776681630017 (0 credits, tier=free).
seed_data_creation: Inserted PRO user (test-user-pro-1776682700000) + session (test_session_pro_1776682700000) via mongo. Created 2 comics (comic_test_test-use) for FREE and ULT users for Reader testing. Cleared prior sub_pro paid payment_transactions for FREE user so promo first-time check passes.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All backend tests in /app/backend/tests/ run together in one pytest invocation (30/30 pass in ~60s). Key seeded users listed above. Frontend features tested: /billing (walking doodle lift + promo INK50 flow), /create (upsell + toggle gating), /comic/:id (PDF button MISSING from UI despite downloadPDF function). Main action: add reader-pdf button to Reader.jsx toolbar then re-test PDF flow for free (toast) and ultimate (jspdf download, no crash).
rca of the issue: reader-pdf button missing: downloadPDF() async function was added to Reader.jsx but the corresponding <button data-testid='reader-pdf'> was never added to the JSX toolbar — likely the developer forgot to render the button after writing the handler. Reproduction: log in as any user, navigate to /comic/{id}, inspect toolbar — you'll see reader-layout-grid, reader-layout-webtoon, reader-edit, reader-download (HTML) but no reader-pdf. Fix: add <button data-testid='reader-pdf' onClick={downloadPDF} disabled={exportingPdf} className='btn-ink !py-2 !px-3 text-sm inline-flex items-center gap-1'><FileDown size={14}/> PDF {!isUltimate && <Crown size={12}/>}</button> after the reader-download button (line ~147).
